back_translation: He made the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood — five cubits its length and five cubits its width, square — and three cubits its height.
He made its horns on its four corners; its horns were of one piece with it. He overlaid it with bronze.
He made all the vessels of the altar — the pots, the shovels, the sprinkling bowls, the meat forks, and the firepans. All its vessels he made of bronze.
He made a grating for the altar, a network of bronze, under its ledge from below up to its middle.
He cast four rings at the four ends of the bronze grating, as holders for the poles.
He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
He inserted the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar to carry it with them. He made it hollow with boards.
He made the basin of bronze and its stand of bronze from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
He made the courtyard: on the south side, hangings for the courtyard of fine twisted linen — one hundred cubits.
Their twenty pillars and their twenty bronze bases — the hooks of the pillars and their bands of silver.
On the north side a hundred cubits — their twenty pillars and their twenty bronze bases. The hooks of the pillars and their bands of silver.
On the west side, hangings of fifty cubits — their ten pillars and their ten bases. The hooks of the pillars and their bands of silver.
On the east side toward the sunrise — fifty cubits.
Hangings of fifteen cubits on the side — their three pillars and their three bases.
On the second side, on this and on this for the gate of the courtyard, hangings of fifteen cubits — their three pillars and their three bases.
All the hangings of the courtyard round about — of fine twisted linen.
The bases for the pillars were bronze; the hooks of the pillars and their bands were silver; the overlay of their tops was silver — and they were banded with silver, all the pillars of the courtyard.

The screen of the gate of the courtyard was the work of an embroiderer — of blue and purple and scarlet yarn and fine twisted linen — twenty cubits in length and the height in width five cubits, corresponding to the hangings of the courtyard.
Their four pillars and their four bronze bases; their hooks of silver, and the overlay of their tops and their bands of silver.
All the pegs for the tabernacle and for the courtyard round about — bronze.
These are the accounts of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the Testimony, which were counted at the command of Moses — the service of the Levites under the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made everything that the LORD commanded Moses.
With him was Oholiab son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan — an engraver and a designer, and an embroiderer in blue and in purple and in scarlet yarn and in fine linen.

All the gold that was used for the work in all the work of the sanctuary — and the gold of the wave offering was twenty-nine talents and seven hundred thirty shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.
And the silver from those numbered of the congregation was one hundred talents, and one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel —

a beka per head — half a shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel — for everyone who crossed over to be numbered, from twenty years of age and upward; for six hundred three thousand five hundred and fifty.
The hundred talents of silver were for casting the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil — one hundred bases for one hundred talents, a talent per base.
And with the one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five he made hooks for the pillars, overlaid their tops, and made bands for them.
And the bronze of the wave offering was seventy talents and two thousand four hundred shekels.
He made with it the bases of the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, and the bronze altar and the bronze grating that was for it, and all the vessels of the altar,
and the bases of the courtyard round about, and the bases of the gate of the courtyard, and all the pegs of the tabernacle and all the pegs of the courtyard round about.
